summary_of_change: Definition retained without substantive change. Wording modernised.
Definition of murder retained. Section number changed from 300 to 101.
Death or life imprisonment retained. New sub-section 103(2) adds aggravated punishment for mob lynching by five or more persons on grounds of race; caste; sex; language or belief.

ipc_title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
bns_title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
summary_of_change: Maximum punishment retained at imprisonment for life. Minimum of five years now mandated for the more serious limb of the offence.
Maximum punishment increased from 2 years to 5 years. New sub-section adds 10-year punishment for hit-and-run drivers who flee without reporting.
Substantively retained. Renumbered.
Definition of theft retained. Snatching is now a separate offence in BNS Section 304.

Maximum imprisonment retained at 3 years. New first-time offender provision: theft of property worth less than five thousand rupees on return of property results in community service rather than imprisonment.
Definition retained.
Maximum punishment of 7 years retained.
Definition retained.
Maximum 10 years retained; extended to 14 years for highway robbery between sunset and sunrise.
Life imprisonment or 10 years rigorous imprisonment retained.
Definition retained.

ipc_title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
bns_title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
summary_of_change: Maximum punishment of 5 years retained (up from 3 years in IPC).
Definition retained.
Maximum 3 years imprisonment retained.

ipc_title: Cheating with knowledge that wrongful loss may ensue
bns_title: Cheating with knowledge of wrongful loss
summary_of_change: Maximum 5 years retained.

ipc_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
bns_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
summary_of_change: Maximum 7 years retained. Same offence; renumbered from the iconic IPC 420 to BNS 318(4).
Definition retained.
Maximum punishment increased from 3 years to 5 years.
Definition retained.
Basic punishment of 2 years retained. Aggravated form (threats of death; grievous hurt; fire; or imputing unchastity to a woman) carries up to 7 years.
Additional 2-year punishment for anonymous threats retained.

ipc_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
bns_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
summary_of_change: Substantively retained.
Definition retained.
Maximum 2 years retained. Community service introduced as an alternative punishment.

bns_title: Act endangering sovereignty unity and integrity of India
summary_of_change: Sedition (IPC 124A) repealed. Replaced by a narrower offence covering acts that excite secession; armed rebellion or subversive activities. Punishment: life imprisonment or up to 7 years and fine.
Substantive definition retained. Age of consent retained at 18 years.
Minimum 10 years retained; may extend to imprisonment for life. Custodial rape (by police; public servant; jail or hospital staff) attracts life imprisonment for the natural life of the convict.

New offence. Covers continuing unlawful activity by organised crime syndicates including kidnapping for ransom; contract killing; cyber-crimes; trafficking; and economic offences. Punishment: death or life imprisonment if it causes death; otherwise 5 years to life and minimum five lakh rupees fine.
New offence in the general criminal code (previously only in special legislation). Punishment: death or life imprisonment if it causes death; otherwise minimum 5 years up to life imprisonment.
